page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 vCPUs, and is paired with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU resources, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of compute and memory capabilities. Single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng yields a score of 1100.09, while multi-core performance reaches 1460.39. Memory bandwidth tests show 81.43 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 10.22 GB/sec for large uncached read operations and 9.28 GB/sec for large uncached write operations. Geekbench scores reflect these constraints, with a single-core score of 911.0 and a multi-core score of 1075.0.

In application-specific workloads, the instance demonstrates limited throughput. Redis SET operations achieve 1.42 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 1.27 GB/sec. Small-scale language model inference using a 135M model processes prompts at 127.07 tokens per second and generates text at 37.1 tokens per second. Given these performance profiles, the ecs.sn2.medium is best suited for low-intensity general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, or lightweight development and testing environments where high-throughput processing is not required.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark evaluations place its compute and memory performance in the lower percentiles, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1075.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 1828.27. Memory bandwidth tests show 10.22 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 9.28 GB/sec for large uncached writes. It delivers a static web serving throughput of 1.27 GB/sec and handles Redis SET operations at 1.42 million operations per second. Given its resource profile and modest performance characteristics, this instance is best suited for low-demand general-purpose tasks, basic web hosting, and lightweight development environments.
